message:
feat(packs): implement confidence tiering for warn-by-default patterns

Implement the confidence tiering system (git_safety_guard-oien.2.2) that allows
Medium severity patterns to warn instead of block by default:

Evaluator changes:
- Remove the skip for non-blocking severity patterns
- All severity levels are now evaluated; policy layer determines final mode

Pattern severity updates (moved to Medium = warn by default):
- containers.docker:image-prune - only affects unused images
- containers.docker:container-prune - only affects stopped containers
- containers.podman:image-prune - only affects unused images
- containers.podman:container-prune - only affects stopped containers
- core.git:branch-force-delete - recoverable via reflog
- core.git:stash-drop - single stash, recoverable via fsck

Test updates:
- Add medium_severity_patterns_are_evaluated test
- Add medium_severity_git_patterns_are_evaluated test
- Add critical_patterns_still_return_critical_severity test
- Add policy_converts_medium_to_warn_mode test
- Update existing severity tests to reflect new classifications
- Replace all_core_rules_block_by_default with core_rules_have_appropriate_severity

This enables the policy layer to convert Medium severity matches to warnings
(DecisionMode::Warn) instead of blocks, reducing friction for recoverable operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -69,17 +69,19 @@ fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
             r"docker\s+network\s+prune",
             "docker network prune removes ALL unused networks."
         ),
-        // image prune - removes unused images
+        // image prune - removes unused images (Medium: only affects unused images)
         destructive_pattern!(
             "image-prune",
             r"docker\s+image\s+prune",
-            "docker image prune removes unused images. Use 'docker images' to review first."
+            "docker image prune removes unused images. Use 'docker images' to review first.",
+            Medium
         ),
-        // container prune - removes stopped containers
+        // container prune - removes stopped containers (Medium: only affects stopped)
         destructive_pattern!(
             "container-prune",
             r"docker\s+container\s+prune",
-            "docker container prune removes ALL stopped containers."
+            "docker container prune removes ALL stopped containers.",
+            Medium
         ),
         // rm -f (force remove containers)
         destructive_pattern!(
